subject: [PATCH 3/3] mm: read remote memory without the mmap lock where possible

reviews.author: Lorenzo Stoakes
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes expressed disagreement with the patch, stating that he doesn't think the changes are necessary and requested further discussion in the review.

Reviewer Lorenzo Stoakes requested that patch descriptions be kept concise and readable by using paragraphs instead of a wall of text.
reviews.raw_body:
As I said in reply to 2/3 I don't think you need to do this.

Let's have a discussion _in the review_ about it :)

---

No wall of text please :)

Same comments for the rest, please use paragraphs, try to be succinct, etc.

2026-06-25.patch_summary: This patch adds an opportunistic, read-only fast path to access remote memory in Linux. The fast path takes the per-VMA lock and copies resident pages out using a page table walk run with interrupts disabled. If the request spans multiple VMAs or if permission checks fail, it falls back to the mmap_lock path for validation. This patch aims to reduce contention on large machines by avoiding unnecessary locking of the mmap lock in common cases where memory is already resident.
